message:
test(fuzz): add fcp-store object_id verifier fuzz target (resolves a4xdb)

Adds fuzz_object_id_verifier covering KeyedObjectIdVerifier::verify,
the WAL-injection gate added by bead 4g0qr (object_id_verifier.rs:18-30).

The existing 4 unit tests cover hand-picked accept/reject classes; this
target exercises arbitrary (zone, header_fields, body, claimed_id, key)
tuples to surface panics on edge-case header serialization and accept-
or reject-path drift across the full input space.

Properties asserted:

  1. Genuine objects (claimed_id == derive_id(header, body, key))
     verify when the key is installed.
  2. VerifierKeyMissing fires when no key for the object's zone.
  3. Forged claimed_id rejected with ContentIdMismatch{claimed=forged,
     computed=genuine}.
  4. Body bit-flip rejected with ContentIdMismatch — the (unchanged)
     claimed id no longer matches the recomputed id.
  5. Wrong-key verifier (same content, different ObjectIdKey) rejects
     with ContentIdMismatch — exercises the keyed-derivation MR.

This is the WAL-injection attack class ContentIdMismatch was designed
to close: a forged Put record with a stolen object_id but attacker
bytes can only land if verify silently accepts; this target ensures
no input combination evades that gate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -890,7 +890,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5mhhv","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-crypto: FrostSigningNonces still duplicate via serde round-trip","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/frost.rs:319-331, 1030-1048

Repro: call commit() to obtain FrostSigningNonces, serialize it with any serde backend, deserialize two copies, then call sign() twice on two different messages with the duplicated nonce package. Removing Clone in 1c52092b only blocks one duplication path; public Serialize/Deserialize still bypass the move-only single-use guarantee the API docs advertise.

Suggested fix: do not expose generic Serialize/Deserialize on FrostSigningNonces. Replace it with an explicit sealed persistence envelope or typestate-backed consume-on-load API that cannot yield two live nonce packages from one serialized blob.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:13.043233Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:49.567801Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:49.567150Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-5zmkr (same FROST serde round-trip finding)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5s19s","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth/token.rs: get_or_refresh rejects still-valid non-refreshable tokens inside proactive refresh window","description":"file: crates/fcp-oauth/src/token.rs:538-550

repro:
1. Store an OAuthTokens snapshot with access_token + token_type present, expires_at about 4 minutes in the future, and no refresh_token.
2. Call TokenStore::get_or_refresh(key, client).
3. The token is still usable, but needs_refresh() is true inside the 5-minute proactive refresh window.
4. get_or_refresh falls through to snapshot.refresh_token().ok_or(OAuthError::NoRefreshToken) and returns an error instead of the still-valid token.

suggested fix:
If authorization material is still present and the token is not actually expired, return the current snapshot when refresh is unavailable. Only require a refresh token once the access token is expired or otherwise unusable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:00.987778Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:22:05.276673Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:22:05.276509Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","title":"TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests (Introspection, Tool Descriptors, Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Agent Integration Unit Tests

## Goal
Verify that FCP2 operations are discoverable and safe for AI agents.

## Test Categories

### 1. Introspection Schema Tests
- Introspection object schema validation (canonical CBOR)
- All required fields present (operations, schemas, risk levels)
- Golden vectors for introspection responses

### 2. Operation Discovery Tests
- Enumerate all connector operations
- Each operation has input/output schemas
- Risk tier correctly reported
- Rate limits correctly reported

### 3. MCP Tool Descriptor Tests
- Mapping to MCP-compatible tool descriptors
- Tool name, description, examples present
- Input/output schema translation
- Risk tier to MCP safety level mapping

### 4. E2E Discovery Tests
- Agent can enumerate tools from connector
- Agent can invoke safe operation end-to-end
- Agent can read recovery hints on failure

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON with operation_id, schema versions
- Discovery timing metrics
- Error details with recovery hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- Introspection is complete for agent operation
- No hardcoded connector knowledge required
- Tool descriptors match MCP conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:32.192813258Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312640984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:34.312513066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent","fcp2","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5szl","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5un1","title":"TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-telemetry Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-telemetry` emits spec-required metrics/logging fields, preserves correlation, and never leaks secrets.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for metrics, logging, and tracing library.

## Test Categories

### Structured Logging
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] JSON output format
- [ ] Field injection (correlation IDs, zone, connector)
- [ ] Sensitive data redaction
- [ ] Log rotation handling

### Metrics Collection
- [ ] Counter increments
- [ ] Gauge values
- [ ] Histogram distributions
- [ ] Timing measurements
- [ ] Label/tag handling

### Distributed Tracing
- [ ] Span creation and nesting
- [ ] Context propagation
- [ ] Trace ID generation
- [ ] Span attributes
- [ ] Sampling decisions

### Export
- [ ] Prometheus format export
- [ ] OTLP format export
- [ ] Custom sink support
- [ ] Batch flushing

### Performance
- [ ] Low overhead in hot path
- [ ] Async export
- [ ] Buffer management

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5vy","title":"fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway","description":"# Inbound Event Gateway Meta-Connector

**Force Multiplier:** Universal webhook receiver for external service integrations.

## Overview

Many services push events via webhooks (GitHub, Stripe, Discord, Slack, etc.). Instead of building separate webhook handlers for each service, this meta-connector provides a unified HTTP endpoint infrastructure with per-service configuration.

## Core Components

### 1. HTTP Endpoint Management
- Dynamic endpoint creation/deletion
- Unique URL per webhook source
- Support for path-based routing (`/webhooks/{service}/{id}`)
- Health check endpoints
- CORS and preflight handling
- Rate limiting per endpoint

### 2. Signature Verification (Per-Service)
- Pluggable verification strategies:
  - HMAC-SHA256 (GitHub, Stripe)
  - HMAC-SHA1 (legacy services)
  - Ed25519 (Discord)
  - API key header
  - Basic auth
  - Custom verification functions
- Timing-safe comparison
- Signature caching for replay prevention
- Failed verification alerting

### 3. Payload Transformation
- JSON schema validation
- Payload normalization to common event format
- Field mapping/extraction
- Nested payload unwrapping
- Binary payload handling (multipart)
- Compression support (gzip, deflate)

### 4. Event Routing to Zones
- Route events to appropriate FCP zones
- Support multiple destinations per event type
- Filter events based on payload content
- Priority-based routing
- Fan-out to multiple handlers

### 5. Retry/Dead-Letter Handling
- Configurable retry strategies:
  - Exponential backoff
  - Fixed interval
  - Fibonacci backoff
- Dead letter queue for failed events
- Manual retry interface
- Event replay capability
- TTL-based expiration

## Configuration Example

```toml
[[webhooks]]
name = "github-repo-events"
path = "/webhooks/github/main-repo"
signature_type = "hmac-sha256"
signature_header = "X-Hub-Signature-256"
secret = "${GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.github-automation"
event_type_header = "X-GitHub-Event"

[[webhooks]]
name = "stripe-payments"
path = "/webhooks/stripe/payments"
signature_type = "stripe"
secret = "${STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET}"
route_to = "zone.billing"
retry_max = 5
retry_backoff = "exponential"

[[webhooks]]
name = "discord-interactions"
path = "/webhooks/discord/bot"
signature_type = "ed25519"
public_key = "${DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY}"
route_to = "zone.discord-bot"
```

## Event Format (Normalized)

```json
{
  "id": "evt_abc123",
  "source": "github",
  "type": "push",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "webhook_id": "github-repo-events",
  "payload": { ... },
  "metadata": {
    "delivery_id": "xyz789",
    "signature_valid": true,
    "received_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.123Z"
  }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Support 10+ common webhook signature types
- [ ] Sub-50ms processing latency
- [ ] 99.9% uptime for webhook endpoints
- [ ] Complete audit trail for all received webhooks
- [ ] Zero data loss with dead-letter queue
- [ ] Hot-reload of webhook configurations

## Dependencies

- HTTP server framework (axum/actix-web)
- Persistent queue for dead-letter handling
- Zone routing system

## Security Considerations

- All secrets stored encrypted
- IP allowlisting per webhook (optional)
- Request size limits
- Timeout handling
- DDoS protection integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:23:06.647234418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T15:53:35.455928212Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-3md (fcp.webhook-receiver: Inbound Event Gateway Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","title":"[fwc] offline preflight should succeed with offline-artifact exit semantics","description":"fwc preflight has an explicit offline-artifact analysis path in crates/fwc/src/main.rs, but preflight_dispatch_without_host currently returns CliExitCode::Transport even when it successfully emits a structured offline analysis payload with CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact. This makes the hybrid degraded path look like a connectivity failure to agents and scripts despite delivering useful output. Acceptance: offline fwc preflight returns success semantics with preserved offline-artifact provenance, and a regression test locks the behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:37:59.574831Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-27T17:54:20.192895Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T17:53:39.245501Z","close_reason":"Already satisfied in current tree by commit f664374c; offline preflight returns success semantics with offline-artifact provenance and existing regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","preflight","truthfulness"],"comments":[{"id":2045,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-5w1wb","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Fresh pass on 2026-03-27: this bead is already satisfied in the current tree. preflight_dispatch_without_host in crates/fwc/src/main.rs now returns CliExitCode::Success and injects CommandAvailability::OfflineArtifact, matching the bead acceptance text. Source history shows commit f664374c fixing this path, and the unit test preflight_offline_shows_risk_and_approval_info already asserts success status plus offline-artifact provenance. I attempted targeted rch exec verification, but rch 1.0.16 hit dependency-preflight RCH-E326 and tried to fail open to local Cargo, which I aborted per repo rules. Closing this bead as stale or already-fixed state. Agent Mail registration remains blocked by the corrupted storage.sqlite3 database refusing automatic recovery while WAL/SHM sidecars are live.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:54:20Z"}]}
@@ -978,7 +978,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-88c9l","title":"conformance: session ack attestation + suite-floor + transcript-binding tests","description":"No conformance test in fcp-conformance/tests/ exercises the responder->initiator MeshSessionAck verify path. session.rs has these inline but cross-crate coverage is missing. Add a session_ack_attestation_conformance.rs that pins the public verify_ack_attested + MeshSessionAck::verify contract: AckHelloMismatch on swapped from/to, AckSuiteNotOffered when ack picks an unoffered suite, AttestationNodeMismatch on identity drift, TimestampSkew on stale ack, InvalidSignature on tampered ack, MissingSignature on unsigned ack, plus a happy-path round-trip via verify_ack_attested.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:23:12.363098Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:03.593744Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:03.593224Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2973,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-88c9l","author":"jemanuel","text":"7614eae8 adds session_ack_attestation_conformance.rs with 10 tests pinning the responder->initiator ack verify path: happy round-trip, AckHelloMismatch on both ack.from/hello.to and ack.to/hello.from drift, AckSuiteNotOffered on unoffered suite, AttestationNodeMismatch on identity drift, TimestampSkew on stale ack, InvalidSignature on tampered sig, MissingSignature on unsigned ack, plus two transcript-binding tests for hello.eph_pubkey and hello.nonce. All 10 pass via rch (cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test session_ack_attestation_conformance).","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-898o4","title":"[REVIEW-A9][P1] connectors/zapier/src/connector.rs:189: configure accepts base_url outside endpoint policy","description":"ZapierConfig::from_params accepts arbitrary base_url and handle_configure constructs ZapierClient before enforcing base_url_policy. provisioning_readiness reports the policy but does not fail configuration, so api_key bearer auth can be routed to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Enforce base_url_policy during configure/from_params, keeping localhost/127.0.0.1 test allowance, and add a regression test for rejected non-Zapier hosts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:44:00.863111Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:46.457849Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:46.457352Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2918,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-898o4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit 63493e6e (cc_2/SunnyMoose). Promoted base_url_policy check to a hard refuse inside ZapierConfig::from_params; the ZapierClient (carrying bearer token) is never constructed for off-policy URLs. Localhost/127.0.0.1 test allowance preserved. 4 new regression tests (rejects evil.example.com, rejects http downgrade, accepts localhost, default URL guard) + 2 existing tests inverted (config_custom_base_url + lifecycle_self_check_bad_network → configure-time refuse). 247 fcp-zapier tests passing. Symmetric to br-n4429 (f17bf191): fail closed at construction, not surface violations as status codes callers may ignore.","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:50:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","title":"[FCP3/P7.3] Produce the final conformance, performance, and operational proof bundle","description":"Background: the migration should end with a proof bundle, not a narrative claim. The project needs one place where conformance, performance, and operational evidence show that the new architecture really exists and remains within project goals.

Scope: assemble the final proof bundle covering semantic ownership, protocol and durable alignment, host and SDK convergence, mesh pilot behavior, CLI truthfulness, deletion backed final architecture shape, and relevant performance and operational characteristics. Explicitly gather the outputs of the preservation tests, cross crate conformance vectors, host backed integration harnesses, mesh failover coverage, CLI acceptance coverage, performance regression harnesses, structured logs, transcripts, and artifact bundles produced by earlier verification beads so the final review is grounded in reproducible evidence rather than summary prose.

Acceptance: cutover can be defended with concrete artifacts, detailed logs, runnable verification scripts, and explicit proof that the final post deletion architecture still satisfies user visible and operator visible requirements.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:09.335001Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:26:31.336936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:26:31.336435Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","performance","phase-7","proof","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:07.870749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:56.898556Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:56.052930Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:06.485857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:03.932820Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:05.411778Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:08.755523Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:57.720816Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","me  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.2","title":"fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Provisioning Automation (OAuth, scopes, devices)

## Goal
Automate Spotify onboarding with minimal human steps while keeping secrets zone-scoped.

## Requirements
- OAuth2 Authorization Code + PKCE via provisioning recipes.
- Scope selection:
  - read-only scopes for search/history
  - separate scopes/capabilities for playback control and library writes
- Device readiness checks:
  - verify the account has at least one available playback device (when required)
  - handle “Premium required” cases explicitly
- Validate NetworkConstraints pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- `fcp doctor` verifies readiness without leaking secrets.
- Mockable in CI.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.3","title":"TEST: Spotify Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Spotify Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)

## Goal
Mock-only tests for fcp.spotify across search, library, playlists, history, and playback control.

## Requirements
- No real Spotify calls in CI.
- Mock endpoints for:
  - search
  - saved tracks/albums/shows
  - playlist CRUD
  - recently played / top items
  - playback control endpoints
- Tests for:
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff
  - premium-required error cases
  - capability gating for playback and write operations
  - polling-backed player state stream behavior (bounded)

## Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic CI suite.
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.4","title":"E2E: Spotify Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Spotify Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Run the shared Mechanical Connector Compliance Runner against fcp.spotify.

## Must verify
- Default deny via CapabilityTokens.
- NetworkConstraints enforcement.
- Receipts/audit for write ops (playlist/library changes, playback control).
- Structured JSON logs with correlation_id + reason codes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner output bundle is green for fcp.spotify.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a6ouw","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/spotify: handshake test masks connector handshake semantics","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/spotify_compliance_e2e.rs:89-120 serializes the real HandshakeRequest but discards the connector response (`let _raw = ...`) and fabricates a HandshakeResponse with fresh session_id, echoed nonce, and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/spotify/src/connector.rs:223-249 only returns protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities. This suite will stay green if the connector handshake stops surfacing required session semantics because the adapter invents them. Fix: assert directly on the connector handshake payload or make the connector emit a real HandshakeResponse-compatible shape and validate that end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:20:23.578854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40.689350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40.688731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2844,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-a6ouw","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in commit c9561961 (by Jeff Emanuel, concurrent sibling commit; my equivalent edit matched the same fix pattern from terraform d18041eb). Adapter now serializes the real HandshakeRequest into handle_handshake, injects a generated session_id, and asserts on the connector-returned contract (protocol_version == 2.0, connector_id present, capabilities array present). capabilities_granted is filtered to only those the connector actually declares, matching production host semantics. Sibling commit also migrated host_allowed to delegate to fcp_sandbox::host_matches_allow_list for consistency with production canonicalization. Note: unable to run 'cargo test -p fcp-e2e --test spotify_compliance_e2e' locally because an unrelated fcp-sandbox build error (egress.rs:811 references canonicalize_host; should be canonicalize_hostname — out of scope for this bead) blocks the workspace build. The handshake code change is textually identical to the shipped fix.","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:03:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a7vd","title":"fcp.mongodb - MongoDB document database connector","description":"## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant MongoDB connector for document CRUD/aggregation and optional change-stream streaming with strict safety and secret handling.

## Credential handling (FCP2)
- Prefer secretless connectors: use `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` and let the egress proxy inject credentials at the network boundary when possible.
- If the protocol requires credential materialization inside the connector (e.g., some DB wire protocols), secrets MUST remain in memory only, be time-bounded, never touch disk, and never appear in logs/errors.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints` (defined in the connector’s manifest/cap map bead if present).
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.
- For DB connectors, constrain raw TCP connects to explicit host:port (or Atlas endpoints) only; deny private ranges unless explicitly allowed by policy.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)

- `storage`
- `operational`
- `streaming`

> Interaction patterns (non-archetype): Database (wire protocol / Atlas Data API) + (optional) change-stream streaming

## Summary

MongoDB connector for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP). This document database connector enables Rust applications to interact with MongoDB clusters and Atlas deployments through type-safe operations, aggregation pipelines, and comprehensive database management capabilities.

## Context

MongoDB is the leading document-oriented NoSQL database, widely used for flexible schema designs, horizontal scaling, and real-time analytics. This connector provides:

- **Document-Oriented Model**: Native BSON document support with flexible schemas
- **Aggregation Framework**: Powerful data processing pipelines
- **Atlas Integration**: Cloud-native MongoDB Atlas Data API support
- **Change Streams**: Real-time data change notifications
- **Sharding Support**: Distributed data across multiple shards

Value Score: 51/100 (Tier 4) - Moderate adoption in Rust ecosystem, strong use case for applications requiring flexible data models.

## Archetype

**Request-Response** - Standard synchronous operations for CRUD, aggregation, and administrative commands.

## Operations

### Read Zone
- `find` - Query documents with filters and projections
- `find_one` - Retrieve single document by query
- `aggregate` - Execute aggregation pipelines
- `count_documents` - Count matching documents
- `distinct` - Get distinct values for a field
- `list_collections` - List all collections in database
- `list_databases` - List all databases on server
- `list_indexes` - List indexes on a collection
- `watch` - Open change stream cursor (streaming context)

### Write Zone
- `insert_one` - Insert single document
- `insert_many` - Bulk insert documents
- `update_one` - Update single document
- `update_many` - Update multiple documents
- `replace_one` - Replace entire document
- `delete_one` - Delete single document
- `delete_many` - Delete multiple documents
- `find_one_and_update` - Atomically find and update
- `find_one_and_replace` - Atomically find and replace
- `find_one_and_delete` - Atomically find and delete
- `bulk_write` - Execute bulk operations

### Admin Zone
- `create_collection` - Create new collection with options
- `drop_collection` - Drop a collection
- `create_index` - Create index on collection
- `drop_index` - Remove index from collection
- `run_command` - Execute raw database command
- `create_database` - Implicitly create database
- `drop_database` - Drop entire database

## Design Notes

### Architecture

```
fcp-mongodb/
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              # Public API exports
│   ├── client.rs           # MongoClient wrapper
│   ├── config.rs           # Configuration types
│   ├── connection.rs       # Connection pool management
│   ├── operations/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── read.rs         # Find, aggregate operations
│   │   ├── write.rs        # Insert, update, delete ops
│   │   └── admin.rs        # Index, collection management
│   ├── atlas/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── data_api.rs     # Atlas Data API client
│   ├── pipeline/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   └── builder.rs      # Aggregation pipeline DSL
│   ├── types/
│   │   ├── mod.rs
│   │   ├── document.rs     # BSON document helpers
│   │   ├── filter.rs       # Query filter builders
│   │   └── options.rs      # Operation options
│   └── error.rs            # Error types
├── Cargo.toml
└── README.md
```

### Connection Configuration

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{MongoConfig, MongoClient};

let config = MongoConfig::builder()
    .endpoint("mongodb+srv://cluster.mongodb.net/") // no credentials in URI
    .auth_credential_id("cred:<mongodb_auth>")      // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .database("myapp")
    .app_name("flywheel-connector")
    .min_pool_size(5)
    .max_pool_size(100)
    .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10))
    .server_selection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
    .retry_writes(true)
    .retry_reads(true)
    .build()?;

let client = MongoClient::connect(config).await?;
```

### Document Operations

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::{doc, Document};
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
struct User {
    #[serde(rename = "_id")]
    id: ObjectId,
    name: String,
    email: String,
    profile: UserProfile,
    tags: Vec<String>,
    created_at: DateTime,
}

// Type-safe operations
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");

// Insert
let user = User { /* ... */ };
let result = users.insert_one(&user).await?;

// Query with filter
let filter = doc! { "email": "user@example.com" };
let user = users.find_one(filter).await?;

// Update with operators
let update = doc! {
    "$set": { "name": "New Name" },
    "$push": { "tags": "verified" }
};
users.update_one(filter, update).await?;
```

### Aggregation Pipeline

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::pipeline::{Pipeline, stage};

let pipeline = Pipeline::new()
    .match_stage(doc! { "status": "active" })
    .lookup(stage::Lookup {
        from: "orders",
        local_field: "user_id",
        foreign_field: "customer_id",
        as_field: "orders",
    })
    .unwind("$orders")
    .group(stage::Group {
        id: "$user_id",
        fields: vec![
            ("total_orders", stage::Accumulator::Sum(1)),
            ("total_spent", stage::Accumulator::Sum("$orders.amount")),
        ],
    })
    .sort(doc! { "total_spent": -1 })
    .limit(10)
    .build();

let results = users.aggregate::<UserStats>(pipeline).await?;
```

### Index Management

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::index::{IndexModel, IndexOptions};

// Create single field index
let index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "email": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .unique(true)
        .name("email_unique")
        .build());
users.create_index(index).await?;

// Create compound index
let compound = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "status": 1, 
    "created_at": -1 
});
users.create_index(compound).await?;

// Create text index for search
let text_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { 
    "name": "text", 
    "description": "text" 
});
users.create_index(text_index).await?;

// Create TTL index
let ttl_index = IndexModel::new(doc! { "expires_at": 1 })
    .options(IndexOptions::builder()
        .expire_after(Duration::from_secs(0))
        .build());
sessions.create_index(ttl_index).await?;
```

### Atlas Data API Support

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::atlas::{AtlasClient, AtlasConfig};

let config = AtlasConfig::builder()
    .base_url("https://data.mongodb-api.com/app/data-xxxxx/endpoint/data/v1")
    .credential_id("cred:<atlas_data_api_key>") // secret reference; never stored on disk
    .data_source("Cluster0")
    .database("myapp")
    .build()?;

let atlas = AtlasClient::new(config);

// Operations work the same way
let users = atlas.collection::<User>("users");
let result = users.find(filter).await?;
```

### Change Streams

```rust
use fcp_mongodb::change_stream::{ChangeStream, ChangeEvent};

let mut stream = users.watch()
    .full_document(FullDocument::UpdateLookup)
    .pipeline(vec![
        doc! { "$match": { "operationType": "insert" } }
    ])
    .start().await?;

while let Some(event) = stream.next().await {
    match event?.operation_type {
        OperationType::Insert => {
            let doc = event.full_document.unwrap();
            println!("New user: {:?}", doc);
        }
        OperationType::Update => { /* ... */ }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

### Transaction Support

```rust
let mut session = client.start_session().await?;

session.start_transaction().await?;

// Operations within transaction
let users = client.collection::<User>("users");
users.insert_one(&user).with_session(&mut session).await?;

let orders = client.collection::<Order>("orders");
orders.insert_one(&order).with_session(&mut session).await?;

session.commit_transaction().await?;
```

### Rate Limiting Integration

```rust
use fcp_ratelimit::{RateLimiter, TokenBucket};

let limiter = RateLimiter::new(TokenBucket::new(
    100,  // requests per second
    Duration::from_secs(1),
));

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .rate_limiter(limiter)
    .build().await?;
```

### Telemetry Integration

```rust
use fcp_telemetry::{Telemetry, SpanKind};

let client = MongoClient::builder()
    .config(config)
    .telemetry(Telemetry::new("fcp-mongodb"))
    .build().await?;

// Operations automatically traced
// Span: mongodb.find { db.name: "myapp", db.collection: "users", ... }
```

## Dependencies

- `fcp-core` - Core FCP traits and types
- `fcp-ratelimit` - Rate limiting for connection management
- `fcp-telemetry` - Observability and tracing
- `mongodb` - Official MongoDB Rust driver (v2.8+)
- `bson` - BSON serialization
- `serde` - Serialization framework
- `tokio` - Async runtime

## Implementation Notes

1. **Driver Choice**: Use the official `mongodb` crate as the foundation, wrapping it with FCP patterns
2. **Connection Pooling**: Leverage the driver's built-in connection pool with configurable sizes
3. **Atlas Data API**: Provide alternative HTTP-based access for serverless/edge deployments
4. **Schema Validation**: Optional JSON Schema validation on collections
5. **Retryable Operations**: Enable retryable reads/writes by default for resilience
6. **Compression**: Support zstd/snappy/zlib compression for network efficiency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Connect to standalone, replica set, and sharded clusters
- [ ] All CRUD operations implemented with type-safe wrappers
- [ ] Aggregation pipeline builder with compile-time validation
- [ ] Index management (create, drop, list)
- [ ] Change streams for real-time updates
- [ ] Transaction support for multi-document ACID
- [ ] Atlas Data API client for serverless scenarios
- [ ] Connection pool metrics exposed via telemetry
- [ ] Rate limiting integration for throttling
- [ ] Comprehensive error types mapping driver errors
- [ ] Integration tests against MongoDB test containers
- [ ] Documentation with examples for all operations

## References

- [MongoDB Rust Driver](https://docs.rs/mongodb/latest/mongodb/)
- [MongoDB Atlas Data API](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api/data-api/)
- [BSON Specification](https://bsonspec.org/)
- [MongoDB Aggregation](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/aggregation/)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-a7vd.1","title":"fcp.mongodb: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.
